reviews.review_description:
Anyone who hates on this game is just low iq trash and don’t understand that it’s still new and a working progress.
This game is dope 
This game will make you wanna become a dungeon crawler 
This game has finger physics you may ask why but all I know is it is pretty awesome 
U can slay goblin lookin things and kill your teammates if you want or team up and kill the other players in the dungeon I mean what more could you want

This game could be cool, but never will be because you have no way of reporting or blocking other players, and anyone can just sneak behind you and kill you without knowing they’re even there because any rando can just join the party. Best of all? No ability to solo it because God forbid someone not want to play with a bunch of mouthy moronic Jerry’s kids.

You earned zero stars and I’ll be reporting your game for lack of reporting systems.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for trying Reave and for taking the time to share your feedback.

Reave is currently in very early limited-access alpha playtests, and these tests exist specifically so we can gather feedback and develop the game alongside the community while many systems are still being built.

Features such as friends lists, blocking, and in-game reporting tools are planned for later stages of development before full release. In the meantime, Meta apps also include a built-in reporting system, players can press the Meta button and select Report, and player names (including their Meta username) are visible above characters so reports can be submitted directly. Both we and Meta take these reports very seriously.

Regarding player interaction: Reave is designed as a PvPvE extraction game, which means other players are intended to be one of the most dangerous threats you’ll encounter in the dungeon. Being attacked by another player is a core part of the gameplay loop, similar to other games in the genre. However, during this playtest we also added a Training mode, where players cannot attack you, allowing new players to learn the systems without PvP pressure.

You may also notice that player characters do not have collision. This is an intentional design choice to prevent “space griefing,” where players could physically block doors, pathways, or other players.

Finally, we understand that shared social spaces can sometimes feel chaotic. As a temporary workaround, players can enter their tent, where only their own character is visible. We are also exploring options like solo or private social instances for players who prefer a quieter space before matchmaking.

We appreciate you giving the playtest a try and sharing your thoughts, feedback like this helps us continue improving the game as development progresses.

reviews.review_description:
Still being worked on I get that. The collision boxes for enemies are almost nonexistent. I understand hard but Making contact with an enemy is critical to gameplay. As well more than a few times I have set my weapon down because I had nowhere to put it. And it disappeared into the void. I was left for the rest of the match to just find a way to get myself killed because I was pinned in an area with no Chest. Need to allow weapons to immediately return to the chest for Quick access of primary weapon. Or allow you to put a primary weapon in your holding slot With your trap and Torch.

Update
Collusion's better. I'm sure About the new progression system, still Likely the best Realistic dungeon crawler on v r

I love this game and it is well polished and thought out (except for blocking). But put that aside it’s a great game. But could you please put a ten dollar in app purchase that allows you to play the game fully in its stage and you are reset when everyone else is able to play. This means you can play whenever you want, not just on the play tests.       I know this is a big ask I just love this game and want to keep playing. Thanks!

This game started with a small idea, and have created a monolith of exploration, dungeon combat and genuine dread. Most games have your enemies rise out of the ground suddenly, with little in terms of lurking fear... this does not! You can plan your strategies and tactics as the situation unfolds... I always liked 'Dungeons', but this is the NEXT LEVEL of true Gritty Fighting and Extraction!

This game is really good for being in earlier stages of development. It has functional combat, unique enemies, a good leveling system, good PvP and extraction features. It does what I assume it wants to do perfectly. The games graphics are really good for an early stage game. I like the amount of physics and the weapons abilities are fun. And the boss is not only unique but challenging.

Updated impression upon March play test.

I had hoped to see improvements and be able to comment on that. Unfortunately it has kinda gone the other way.

Performance is still not good. I didn't get the memory error but it's still fairly choppy.
Clunky inventory and disappearing weapons are still an issue.
Progress from a previous attempt was lost. Not a huge issue however it does defeat the purpose of having a progression system.
Upon entering the training dungeon there was a chest with a crossbow in it. My other weapons disappeared after I grabbed it and it didn't do anything. It wouldn't fire, there was no working reload mechanism etc.
I still have that weird view of the board on the backpack.

Despite seemingly no meaningful improvement in any previously problematic areas, a micro transaction system has been introduced, where one can expect to pay up to £40 for in game currency. 

I'm honestly pretty disgusted by this being added. Not just as someone who doesn't want to see this in games, but by the fact that this is given priority over game fixes. This puts the game down a few notches for me and I don't have as much interest in it anymore. 

****
I appreciate this is early access so hope to see the game come along but the performance of the game was very poor when I tried it. It's struggling to run and ended up forcing a low memory error, despite being fairly low res.

The dungeon 'lobby' was a bit confusing, in that I was standing in the room reading some info with no idea what was actually going on and how to join a dungeon. But as it turns out just being there attempts to join a dungeon and it just failed on first attempt. However I couldn't just walk back to the camp and had to use the menu to exit.

Once in the dungeon it was pretty slow and frustrating between the character mobility and the sluggish framerate, and at one point opening a door caused my sword to completely disappear, so I was stuck with no way to defend myself, and as it turns out, no option to exit the dungeon.

Whilst looking for the sword in my holster, looking down resulted in seeing what looks like a wooden board which I'm guessing is a placeholder for the backpack or something. But it kept jumping around me when I looked left and right, along with my hip holster slots.  It's really distracting and made trying to figure out what was going on with the missing sword unnecessarily awkward. Something that could be doing with being removed altogether, or made to stay fixed.

These are just general first impressions concerning some issues I encountered. Putting that aside this has the underpinnings of a very impressive dungeon crawler with obvious inspiration from classic games. The dungeons and creatures look good, there's a comprehensive if not necessarily intuitive inventory,  vendors for potions and weapons etc, and I'm sure if it ran smoothly and the character is levelled up so as to not feel underpowered it could get to be really interesting to play.

TL;DR - I can't recommend it based on performance right now but it has huge potential and may well turn out to be one of the best games on quest if the Devs can tidy it up and improve performance.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks a lot for taking the time to update your review and write all of this out, we really do appreciate it. It’s especially helpful when players come back and share how things feel across multiple playtests. On the performance side, we’re a bit surprised to hear you’re still experiencing issues, so it would really help to understand what headset you’re playing on. We’ve tested extensively on Quest 3 and 3S and are consistently seeing around 70 to 72 FPS during normal gameplay, which is also reflected in our wider player metrics and stats from Meta directly. If you are on Quest 2, the game isn’t officially supported there right now, which can lead to the kind of choppiness and instability you’re describing. If you are on Quest 3 or 3S and still seeing this, we would really like to investigate further because that is not what we are seeing internally or across most players, and the best way for us to do that is if you can share a short clip or steps to reproduce via our Discord so the team can dig into it properly.

On the inventory and disappearing weapons, this is definitely something we would want to fix quickly, but at the moment it is not something we are able to reproduce on our side and we are not seeing widespread reports of it from other players. Issues like weapons disappearing, crossbows not functioning, or items behaving incorrectly are all things we can address once we can reliably reproduce them, but without footage or steps it is very difficult for us to track down what is actually happening. The same applies to the backpack or board issue you mentioned, we are not entirely sure what you are seeing there so it is hard to diagnose without being able to see it in action. If you are able to join our discord and share a clip of that happening in a support ticket it would help us massively in figuring out what is going wrong. It is likely without footage/reproducible steps to make these things happen, we won't b able to fix them as they are not known issues. We are aware that sometimes when you let go of a weapon it can instantly be sent to your bag but there are no reports of weapons disappearing entirely while in raid.

Regarding progression, that is a fair point and even if it did not feel like a huge issue it is still something that should feel consistent and reliable. We do perform wipes between major playtest phases while systems are still being actively developed, but we understand that this can feel frustrating and it is something we are continuing to improve both in terms of how it works and how clearly it is communicated.

On microtransactions, we completely understand the reaction and it is something people feel strongly about, but just to clarify, Reave is a free to play game and will remain free to play, there are no plans to charge for the game itself. The currency can also be earned in game every day, and the system itself was introduced back in December rather than being something newly prioritised over fixes, it has beeen around for 4 months now. That said, if it gives the impression that monetisation is coming before stability or polish then that is on us to communicate better and we will take that on board.

You also mentioned confusion around entering dungeons in your earlier experience, and we have made changes there in the March playtest with clearer UI, matchmaking flow, and difficulty selection, although it is important for us to hear if those improvements did not land clearly. Overall we agree with your closing thoughts, the foundation is there but the experience needs to meet that potential, and right now the main challenge for us with the issues you have described is that we cannot reproduce them on our side. If you are open to it, joining the Discord and sharing even a short clip would allow us to investigate properly and get fixes in place much faster. Thanks again for taking the time to write this out and we hope you will check back in as we continue improving things.

reviews.review_description:
So I believe I'm allowed to now say that I have been following this game's development since its earliest stages. I've watched it evolve from a nameless concept with 2 skeleton types to its current form, and the Alta guys have really done something great!

The PvPvE Extraction gente is quite a unique and fun one. The best example I can think of is for Android called Gold And Glory. The concept is mostly simple. There is a dungeon filled with treasures and gear. Your objective is to find as much of that treasure as you can and leave the dungeon with it. You only need to open a portal to extract (leave) safely! There's a few of those portals in this dungeon. You need to find one. And do it quickly, because there is a darkness that will expand further the longer you're in the dungeon. That expansion is outward in, though, squeezing tighter, closing in until the entire dungeon is consumed. If you're caught in this fog, it will suck the life from you without prejudice, draining your health endlessly until nothing remains. The longer you stay, the smaller the safe zone becomes. Of course, the darkness isn't the only thing trying to kill you. The dungeon is full of monsters that have been far too lonely for far too long. They don't want you to leave. You're their entertainment!

I hope you didn't think you were the only player interested in that treasure, btw. Other players, just like you, found their own way in, and they are coming for that treasure, too. Everything you take is something they can't. That's a pretty big incentive for them to not let you leave with anything! As the dungeon squeezes you closer into the same space, you should expect to run into them. You're all ready to leave and take your rewards, and it's getting pretty cramped. If you kill them, you can have their loot, too. They're considering the same thing about you, though. Oh, and there's another player. Maybe you should have left earlier, but would you even feel satisfied with the few things you had at that time? Do you rush for the portal and try to stay alive long enough to get through it? Or do you hope that these guys will attack those guys, and they'll be distracted so you can leave. But then you remember that they'll be trying to kill each other. Surely you can let them nearly kill each other and strike to take ALL the loot as yours. You better decide because the darkness is just behind you, and it's moving. You wait. The other two are fighting! Now's your chance to take it all! But just as you charge in for the kills, you catch a glimpse of yet another person. He knows your plan and you've been seen by the other guys who are now prepared for your attack. And as you kill the last of the original players, you take a nasty blow. That hurt a bit. And that last player noticed that, too. He's charging at you now, as you struggle to lift your sword again to block the charging threat, you realize he's not struggling. And as you fall to the floor in front of the portal, you realize you won't be physically leaving. No prize for you. Maybe next time you'll leave earlier. Or maybe you won't...

I just hate how when you die you lose everything you had even in other runs. You gain good loot and die just to go back to scratch.  This game also doesn't let you do other features like blocking or actually running.

Reminds me of dark and darker but in vr. it is very buggy at the moment and there isn't a while lot but it is still being developed and for what it is rn it's really fun. The game has a lot of potential.

aright, i am a bit sour towards alta for neglecting ATT when it could be one of the absolute best games in vr. However i did give this an honest try to see what they deemed more important and, i must say i am disappointed. This could have potential but it honestly is not different enough from every other dungeon crawler game out there. Yes it does have a few new elements to it but township was unique and incredible, if this was the direction alta wanted then the mines could have been reworked for att or something else added in a new area. Att was unlike any other and didn't deserve the disrespect of this removing it from its throne. This games avatars are quite unsettling and this really needs a lot more work and more unique mechanics to separate this from others before it should be available for early playtesting. If i want to kill monsters in a dungeon ill play one already released as ancient dungeon or dungeons of eternity. Not going to sit and wait for this game when, if it gets good the devs or head honcho calling the shots will just throw it out the window for the next thing they come up with. RIP A Township Tale. If only someone else had you and could nurture your potential, perhaps you could have continued to grow to something even more incredible.

developer_response.body:
Reave is still in limited-access playtests, so it’s early and very much evolving. If it’s not your kind of game, that’s completely okay, but if you ever want to try it during a test and share feedback, we’re listening.  Reave isn’t meant to “replace” A Township Tale, and it’s not something we expect everyone to be excited abou, it’s a different game with a different focus. We’re continuing development on Reave with the small team we have, and we’re trying to build something worth your time in VR.

We know people care deeply about Township Tale. Reave exists because we wanted to try something new and challenging, not because we stopped believing in past work. 

Thanks for caring enough to speak up. Even strong reactions come from a place of love for VR, and we don’t take that lightly.

The games amazing and i absolutely cant wait for the mage class to come out!!!! However the issue im having is that you guys completely abandoned ATT which could have been an absolutely fantastic game but you guys decide to ditch it for this game. The game is still amazing though keep up the good work and maybe work on ATT? Thanks

Feels like Dark and Darker for VR. I like the trap placements but please add more variety and dungeon mechanics. 

I like the slow, cautious and careful approach on dungeons which feels tense and dangerous. It separates this game from other dungeon crawlers where you can just sprint and blast through from start to end with arcade-y feels.

I'd def welcome any survival mechanics like hunger and status management like bleeding/poison etc for a more hardcore and punishing approach.

This is a pretty outstanding game I found on a limb, but after 10 to 12 raids the map being the exact same every time gets boring same with having almost no enemy variety. I would love this game more than I already do if it just got some more than much needed content.

reviews.review_title: Filled a Huge Gap in the Market
reviews.review_description:
My two brothers and I try to log in once a week to play VR. We've completed a lot of games together; Guardians, Zero Caliber, and many more. Our go-to game is Contractors.

Reave is the sort of game we have wanted for a long time, but it hasn't existed. A medieval sword style online game of this kind was completely missing from the market.

We played for hours last night, and we are hooked. We've bought better armour using gold from loots and levelled up our characters.

Bring the full game! I cannot wait to support your team by buying the game, and I cannot wait to see what the full release brings.

You guys have smashed it
It was a fun journey
Super gra
Bro the tut is so hard for no reason make it easier
This game has so much potential i love it so much keep up thr great work. 😀👌
Loved this game so sad to see it go, RIP Reave

Reave is similar to dungeons of eternity in a lot of ways: It has deeper progression than dungeons of eternity however; it has far less polish than dungeons of eternity. Far more bugs and far earlier￼ on in development. It is also far less forgiving. But it is free so if you have the time you should try it out. ￼

developer_response.body: Reave is still in alpha, not even beta yet so you're totally right about it not being finished yet/earlier in development. I hope you keep coming back and checking in with Reave as we continue to develop it. Feel free to leave any ideas and things you'd like to see on Discord too! We're always listening and updating things based on player feedback!

reviews.review_description: Me and my mate just hopped on for a few hours and honestly this is the most fun VR game we have played for ages!!. We used to play VR all of the time but got so bored and sopped playing entirely but I think this is the game that will be getting us back into it. Super refreshing and fun, saw lots of comments talking about bugs but we didn't encounter any. 10/10

I've always wanted a rpg type game with good combat and good progression and this is perfect. The high stakes of death make the game so much more fun than any other game. I cant wait for more updates!

I cant believe they scraped ATT for this game, dungeons of eternity is better. (although it does cost money) its so buggy, I cant say much about the bugs tho, it is in limited access to be fair. A Township Tale was soooo much better, please, PLEEEAASEE. Don't scrap ATT.

Sword vanished first raid. Hand movement is slow, laggy, and makes combat clunky. Move speed feels like crawling, even while sprinting. No way to adjust holster position, so sitting in a chair while playing is awkward. Weapons seem to tend to fall to the floor randomly.

developer_response.body:
The game is in limited access alpha playtests, this is not the final game. We'd love for you to come back in 2-3 years when the game is nearer a finished state and try again. The game is not in beta or early access yet.

What you experienced is an early, still heavily in development version of the game.

We're also updating the game with more accessibility options and settings but the game is intended (currently) to be played stood up. Later in development, we will look at things like altering holster positions and more seated options. The game is here for playtesting and giving us feedback so we can improve, not to be looked at and seen as a final game that is still many years away. Also at level 1 you are slow but by level 10 you are blazing fast and agile, the game is about progression, leveling up, getting loot and growing skill.

reviews.review_description: Ok so i played for like five hours and first the graphics are like your attempting to have good graphics but it goes dow into the uncanny vally second the gameplay is so hard to get used to and keeping loot you find is near impossible third alright fine ill admit it im slightly biased since i loved att but come on the fact this can even be called ok is disturbing to the proggresion is so hard and not to mention the monsters animations

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to play and share your thoughts. We understand that Reave can feel overwhelming at first, especially coming from a different style of game like ATT.

Reave is intentionally designed as a brutal, hard, and punishing dark fantasy experience. Progression is meant to be earned slowly through risk and repetition, not rushed. Early on, we strongly recommend doing smaller, incremental raids close to the surface to build up levels, learn enemy behavior, and secure basic gear before pushing deeper. Trying to go too far too fast will almost always result in lost loot and frustration.

The difficulty curve, harsh penalties, and unsettling presentation are all deliberate parts of the game’s identity. Reave is not meant to be comfortable or immediately accessible. It is meant to reward patience, mastery, and careful decision making over time.

That said, we appreciate the feedback on visuals, animations, and onboarding. Reave is still evolving, and community input helps us refine clarity, balance, and polish while staying true to the core vision. If you ever decide to give it another shot with a slower, more cautious approach, we think the experience may click differently. 

Thanks again for trying Reave and for being honest about your experience.

reviews.review_description: It's a great game a lot like dark and darker but it would be nice if they added roles and more enemies and the blocking is a little weird but that's probably just a skill issue
Man's history becomes a rat labyrinth.

Still there are a lot of bugs that I won’t even name but when the full version releases I will be sure to play this with my friend(please add a toggle for friendly fire😅)  And please add more weapons.

reviews.review_title: Why won't it let me play
reviews.review_description: I want to play!!!!!!!!!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi there, Reave is in limited access and Alpha stage of development so we currently do playtests once a month for a week or two.

March was a long one, it ran from March 5th to 23rd. The team is hard at work on the updates for April and we’ll have announcements later in the month of when you can next play.

Once you get the chance to play, we'd love it if you could update your review. Many thanks.

The games is a fun game and graphics good and payed games look trash compered to this.
The proplems with this is trying to get my friend to downlode this is hard and if you exit with a wepon in hand it disappears and i remember the game dexided no and made it impossible to exit but overall a good game
Parties are small and not much direction very confusing and you feel alone and very empty, makes no sense

developer_response.body:
Thanks for trying Reave and for taking the time to leave feedback, we really appreciate you giving the game a shot.

Reave is currently in very early limited-access alpha playtests. These are not beta or Early Access builds; they are temporary test periods that allow us to gather feedback and develop the game alongside the community as systems and content are still being built.

Right now the game includes a number of tutorial panels that introduce core mechanics, but we do not yet have a full in-game tutorial experience. On our Discord server we currently host full tutorial videos, walkthroughs, and guides to help players learn the systems during these early playtests. A proper in-game onboarding experience is something we expect to introduce later in development.

Regarding parties: groups are intentionally capped at 3 players, which is the number the game is balanced around. However, when you enter a raid there can actually be up to 11 other players in the dungeon with you, which can make encounters much more chaotic and unpredictable when the instance is full.

At its core, Reave focuses on challenging encounters and progression through the dungeon, starting with smaller enemies and gradually facing tougher foes as you push deeper and earn better loot.

Thanks again for trying the playtest and sharing your thoughts, feedback like this genuinely helps us shape the game as development continues.

reviews.review_description:
If you enjoy challenging dungeon crawlers and slaying creatures creeping around in the dark for loot and weapons, then this game is for you!!!    
You will enter a labyrinth of dungeon areas populated with many creatures ready to attack you, and chests full of treasure!  You must slay them for their essence and avoid being killed by them or another player looking to enrich themselves.  Grab what loot and weapons you can, and extract back to camp to sell your loot and level up!
This game has MASSIVE potential!!!  Reave is early access and still in progress with very active and friendly developers open to suggestions. 
I play many games and this is one of my favorites to come along in quite some time, as well as many of my guildies from TSM. 
If you are looking for a challenge, and have the fortitude I highly recommend this game.
Needs customization and I need a release date PLEASE

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for playing and for the review, really glad you’re enjoying the game.

On customization, this is definitely something we’re actively working on. We’ve got a range of systems coming that will let you change your appearance including hair, facial hair, colours, and more, alongside a growing number of armor sets that will play a big part in defining your look and overall style in the game.

As for release, we’re currently targeting later in 2026 for a wider early access period. But a full release will be years away as we're still in Alpha. We’re continuing to run regular playtests while we build out features and polish the experience, so there’s still a lot more to come.

If you want to keep up with everything we’re adding, we share updates and previews regularly over on the Discord.

Killing enemies are very hard because the damage system, sometimes you deal get big damage or low ￼damage￼. (maybe it’s the stamina system) the player models are ugly, The game is very hard, and I know you abandoned a game. ￼￼overall,￼ 1 star.

developer_response.body:
Hey, appreciate you giving the test a shot and taking the time to leave feedback.

On difficulty: Reave is designed to be challenging, but we’re still tuning the balance heavily. A lot of what you experienced is part of that ongoing iteration, not the final feel.

Development is active, updates are rolling out regularly, and community feedback from tests like this directly shapes what changes next.

Totally fair if the game isn’t for you right now, but we do appreciate you trying it early. If you ever decide to check back in later, it’ll be a very different experience as the months go on and development continues.

developer_response.body:
Reave is still in limited-access playtests, so it’s early and very much evolving. If it’s not your kind of game, that’s completely okay, but if you ever want to try it during a test and share feedback, we’re listening.  Reave isn’t meant to “replace” A Township Tale, and it’s not something we expect everyone to be excited abou, it’s a different game with a different focus. We’re continuing development on Reave with the small team we have, and we’re trying to build something worth your time in VR.

We know people care deeply about Township Tale. Reave exists because we wanted to try something new and challenging, not because we stopped believing in past work. 

Thanks for caring enough to speak up. Even strong reactions come from a place of love for VR, and we don’t take that lightly.

reviews.review_title: 5 star title, with game breaking bugs
reviews.review_description:
Hey kids! How would you like the PvPvE dungeon crawling game that involves monsters, magic, and hero melee fighting? 

Lo and behold! Comes in Reave. Finally, the extraction game doesn't involve modern firearms and body armor, but dungeon crawling and skill, stat level up system. Except....

It is riddled with bugs. Most of them are harmless (such as no ergonomics for the left handed players, bad calibration, weapons held in weird positions) but one of the most breaking obes - THE GAME WILL RANDOMLY GO BLANK AND KICKS YOU OUT OF THE MATCH, AUTOMATICALLY KILLING YOU! 

That means anything you looted there for almost an entire hour of adventure and all the dungeon preparation gear you had to equip from your stash, AND ALL OF THE GOLD EARNED IS GONE! NOT BECAUSE OF THE SKILL ISSUE, BUT BECAUSE OF THE GAME MATCH CRASHES! 

Now, when this all works and you got lucky to play without crashes, how is it?

I would give it 5 stars. This is exactly what a lot of VR gamers had been waiting for for years! D and D style dungeon PvPvE crawler akin to Shadow Hunt. In it you will die a lot. Most of the beginning players don't even make the successful Dungeon Run by at least level 5. If you die, you lose everything, except what you had in your stash (this is why 4 stars had been lopped off, since YOU WILL DIE A LOT BUT NOT TO MONSTERS OR OTHER PLAYERS, BUT TO THE CRASHING BUGS - otherwise the game is amazing).

The goal of the dungeon run is quite well set up. You start out and can extract from the same entry point. To exit, you need to collect monster souls (60 minimum at this Alpha).

The first small monsters are relatively  easy to defeat. Just a few steps in, the humanoid sized ones werebat looking creatures are extremely difficult to defeat, until level 5. However, past level 5 and the sword, they can be taken on one on one.

So what does this early access needs to make it 5 stars game?

1. GET THE SERVER CRASH ISSUES FIXED IMMEDIATELY.
2. Controller swap for left-handed and ambidextrous players.
3.Respec system that costs a lot.
4. Grip angles changed from the asinine Hammer Grip to the Handshake Grip style. Allow handgrip alternate with the press of an A or B keys on either controller, depending in which the weapon held.
5. Polearms and two-handed weapons.
6. Add parry (NOT THE BLOCKING) mechanics that use a lot less of stamina to block the attacks by redirecting the strikes.
7. Add cutting damage to weapons and not just stabbing and chopping damage.
8. FIX THE BUG WHERE PLAYER BEING EXTRACTED, LOSES WEAPONS HELD IN THEIR HANDS. DUE TO GAME CRASHING, ON NUMEROUS OCASSIONS IT AUTO EXTRACTS PLAYERS AND THEY LOSE THEIR WEAPONS HELD IN THEIR HANDS!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to write such a detailed review and for calling out what works as well as what does not. Firstly, the game should not be causing you to lose your stuff when a server prematurely ends/crashes - if this is making you lose your stuff please report via a support ticket on the discord server as we are not aware of this... unless the 20minute raid timer is running out you should not be losing all your stuff in a raid unless you die. 

You are absolutely right that Reave is currently in limited access playtesting and stability issues like unexpected disconnects or crashes are not acceptable experiences, especially in an extraction game where loss matters. Crashes that remove players from a raid and cause loss of gear or progress are a top priority for the team and actively being investigated and fixed during these playtests.

Reave is not a finished release and these limited access periods exist specifically to uncover problems like server instability, calibration issues, and control limitations before full launch. Feedback like yours helps us identify where the experience is breaking down and where it needs improvement most.

We also appreciate you highlighting what the game gets right when things are working. Hearing that the core dungeon crawling, PvPvE structure, progression, and fantasy combat are what you have been waiting for means a lot to the team and reinforces the direction we are building toward.

Many of the points you raised such as left handed support, grip options, parry mechanics, additional weapon types, and deeper combat systems are already under discussion or planned for future development, though they are not all ready during this early phase.

If you are willing, we would really encourage you to join our Discord at discord.gg/Reave and share any crash details or logs you may have. That allows us to track issues more precisely and helps ensure fixes reach players faster.

We understand why this experience led to a one star review, but we hope as stability improves and features expand you might consider checking back in and updating your rating in the future.

reviews.review_description: Sorry about the old review and do you know when it will be early access it is just I want to play it know but can’t so yea that’s what I meant

developer_response.body:
Playtests are every month - the game is available now to play :) 

You do not have to pay at all, this is completely false information. Reave is free to play. But as you can see limited access and read the description it says we have monthly playtests when everyone can play. The game is in Alpha not even Beta or Early Access yet. 

Reave will be playable (yes for free) every month for 1 week, and in March it will be playable from the 5th. We'd love it if you could give the game a go then and change your review based on that.

I am unsure where you got the info that you have to pay to play Reave as you absolutely do not.

That was the first game I ever played on vr and it is the most respected game I have ever played on vr to this day.Nothing has ever come anywhere near it i hope you guys keep it up. the game has very good potential but you need to add classes and certain abilities and for a monster idea you should make a giant fat guy has pretty good defense but low attack

developer_response.body:
Classes are already in! It's on the skill tree - everyone starts as a recruit but depending on the path you take you can become a Fighter or Rogue. And then down those skill trees you can become a Knight, Swordsman, Assassin or Ranger. We're doing a much larger skill and levelling overhaul over the next few months so you'll see even more playstyles and choices when you level up soon! 

Thanks so much for playing Reave and I hope you keep returning every playtest and checking out all the new things!

reviews.review_description: I saw the ads and thought it would be interesting, but then I played it, and there was literally one type of monster and I was just running around with one type of sword. There was no chests or anything.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave a review and for giving Reave a try, we really appreciate it, even when the experience didn’t land for you.

We just want to clarify a bit, in case it helps. In the current build, there are 10+ different creature types and hundreds of loot chests scattered throughout each raid, along with multiple weapon types and gear paths. It sounds like you may have had a very short run, an unlucky early encounter, or didn’t get the chance to explore very far before exiting.

Reave is designed around exploration and risk-reward progression, so the variety opens up more the deeper you push into a raid.

If you’re open to it, we’d genuinely love for you to give it another shot in a future playtest, a lot has already changed, and there’s much more to discover once you get past the very early moments. Thanks again for the feedback, and we hope we can win you over next time.

reviews.review_description:
Cause if I'm right many things will be wiped after the Playtest running while I write this I didn't play long. My experience wasn't 100 % perfect but really cool, a nice Player helped me a bit. I have really high hopes for this game, a fantasy-stile Extraction-Game. 

Devs, please add enough content to keep me playing this a while and I'm almost sure I'll be happy.

I love the game very difficult but I like it more than ghosts of tabor. I like how if I die I can go right back in with decent enough items and if you even make it out once you will be smothered with riches very fun. But is not beginner friendly at all and would benefit from a tutorial.

Last weekend, I played this during limited access. Many shops in the lobby were familiar to me as the typical RPG style and I started by choosing a map for new users. Because of the time zone, I heard only Asian voices in the lobby and it was single player dungeon crawler. I needed time to notice that I had to stood still in the entering room to start a mission. I wish there were some auxiliary elements or guidance like a proper timer till start.
The dungeon of random generation was complicated in length, and the enemy's movement was quite like real, keeping the traditional RPG atmosphere alive. It was a map for beginners, but it was difficult to reach the end without careful progress. Farming and upgrades seem to be desperately needed, and it will be a lot smoother if you work with your colleagues,
Private dungeons are not yet supported, but according to the developer, they are considering it.
There have been some bugs in physical interactions, but it's a fairly anticipated game as a multiplayer authentic RPG that's been rare lately.

The game is really good, but early on I was struggling BADLY. The game is really good. Just focus more development on the tutorial so people won't die and leave a bad review

I got a Potion idea for this game: Recovery potions
It reduces block cooldown temporarily.

Also, something to add to the discord.
Split up ideas and feedback. Don't just stack em up in 1 channel

You need to add a level system because If a level 5 is going against a level 15 there isn’t even a chance of that level 5 surviving that fight 

When you use the settings to adjust your hand the bar you use jumps from end to end whenever you try and adjust you hands 🙌 

Other than that great game I’m sure there’s more I could have said but that’s it for now

The game is still in development, so still needs a few little fixes but just going by the beautiful physics and graphics, I can tell this will be a great game to be had as updates roll in.
Great job developers 👍 keep it up.
I look forward, to see what you've got!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave feedback, even though the experience was clearly frustrating.

Reave is currently in limited access playtesting and is not a finished or polished release. These playtests are live specifically so we can surface bugs like the one you described, fix them, and improve stability before full release. Issues such as falling out of the map or getting stuck after an item interaction are not intended and are exactly the kinds of problems we are actively working to resolve.

If you are willing, we would really appreciate you joining our Discord at discord.gg/Reave and sharing a few details about what happened. That lets the team investigate the issue directly and helps prevent it from happening to other players.

We understand if this experience left a bad impression, but please keep in mind that limited access means the game is still very much in development. As fixes roll out and systems are improved, we hope you might consider checking back in the future.

reviews.review_description:
Really fun and the gameplay is good, just some bugs i’ve had that were pretty bad

2. lagging out and dying to creatures when you can’t fight back

it would also be cool if the stash had more space, right now you can only have about 15 items in your stash at the most

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the detailed review, we’re really glad you’re enjoying the core gameplay.

Reave is still in Limited Access, and the issues you mentioned are things we’re actively working on. Collision problems (like getting pushed through floors or walls) and desync/lag leading to unfair deaths are known pain points, and stability improvements are a big focus for us right now.

The stash size feedback is also noted, the team has some goals for revamping the Stash in the future which would including unlocking additional stash tabs.

If you’re able to share details or clips of these bugs in our Discord, it genuinely helps us track and fix them faster. Thanks again for playing and helping shape the game, we hope you’ll stick with us as things improve.

reviews.review_description: While i gather its still early doors, me and my bro got annoyed with this. Wr spent a while trying to figure it out. When we did we started buying stuff or surviving with gear.   after a while we kept encountering other users when it was just us two in a party. They killed me nicked all mybgear then killed my bro and nicked his. Not going back to waste time on that again.  anyway atmosphetically we like the gloominess of the dark dungeons and found it a little thrill. Ill see how it progresses in time.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave a review and for being honest about your experience.

What you described is unfortunately a core possibility of Reave’s PvPvE extraction gameplay, other players can engage you, defeat you, and take your gear. That risk/reward tension is central to the loop. That said, we fully understand how frustrating it can feel, especially early on when you’re still learning the systems or playing as a small party.

Reave is currently in Limited Access, and we’re actively working on improvements to address exactly this kind of experience. Planned changes include:

Better safeguards to prevent high-level players from freely steamrolling newer or lower-geared players

Longer time-to-kill, so encounters feel more survivable and give players time to react, escape, or fight back

Ongoing tuning to matchmaking, balance, and progression pacing

We’re really glad to hear you enjoyed the atmosphere and the tension of the dark dungeons,that sense of dread and thrill is something we care deeply about preserving as the game evolves.

If you’re open to it, we hope you’ll consider checking back in later in 2026 as these systems mature. Your feedback genuinely helps shape where Reave is going.

Thanks again for giving it a try.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words. Snap turn is a high-priority quality of life feature for us going into the new year. We know how important it is for comfort for specific players in VR. It’s on our roadmap and we’ll share updates as soon as it’s ready.

reviews.review_description:
Issue in raids causing my sword (right hip slot) to randomly disappear, sometimes drop. 

Sometimes I will reach for my sword and instead of pulling my sword from the right hip slot, it pulls out cross bolt bundles. Then I hear a clattering like sword dropping, but it disappears. One time I backtracked and found it at the last chest I opened. Other times it is just gone and it ends up killing me if I haven't found any loot weapons yet. 

Only happens if I have the crossbow bolt bundles in my inventory.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for taking the time to leave a review and for putting real hours into playing Reave. We really appreciate you engaging with the game and sharing your experience.

Your frustration here is completely understandable. Losing gear outside of death or intentionally dropping it is not intended behavior at all, especially when it is an item you worked hard to earn. Issues like that are exactly what we want to identify and fix during Limited Access playtests.

We have not seen this reported very often yet, which makes it harder to track down, but that also means your feedback is especially valuable. If you are able to record a clip or note what was happening right before the item disappeared, such as opening a chest or interacting with inventory, and share it with us on our Discord, it would help the dev team investigate much faster.

Reave is still actively in development, and reports like this help us make sure problems are resolved before we move into full Early Access. Sorry this impacted your experience, and thank you again for taking the time to call it out.

reviews.review_description: This feel like a souls game

reviews.review_title: It’s great.. but needs a single player
reviews.review_description: Every time I play it there is some max level guy killing me. At least put in skill based matchmaking but not like cod’s

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to review and play Reave.

Reave is still in Limited Access Playtesting and we agree that running into very high level players too early can be frustrating. The team is actively looking into level based matchmaking so that, for example, a level 20 player would not be placed into the same raid as a level 1. That kind of separation should help make early runs feel fairer and more approachable.

We are also exploring additional ideas and approaches to matchmaking beyond just levels as development continues. Feedback like this is extremely helpful while we refine progression and the overall player experience.

Fans of dungeons of eternity or battle talent should check this one out. It is really good and offers some really unique gameplay.

My initial impression of this game was severely negative, I heard this is the game that the development of township tale was halted for. Being a huge fan of A township tale I was immediately upset that it was sacrificed for another dungeon crawler game, given there are numerous others. Honestly, this one’s not all that special, minus the PvP and rouge like aspects. 

What really sold this for me was the rouge, the stealth and backstabbing mechanics are uniquely satisfying and exciting. Hunting down other players just for fun is probably my favorite part of the game. 

Though initially the game was very hard, I’d argue it get exponentially easier, while leveling up and the requirements to beat quest become exponentially harder.

All in all, I’m not so disappointed in it, and will definitely be buying it, hopefully it’s not free because I hate kids as easy as they are to fight,  upon its full release.


Also, crap ton of bugs, fix them.
THANK YOU! Ive waited so much for a game like this. Its still alpha, but already promises so much!

But apparenty theyve severely down graded, the charcter looks like deflated pennywise enemies are stupid op you unbearably weak hp wise running isnt even running and your given 0 armour not even a shield no nothing just a sword light and bag and for clothes just rags with 0 defence oh and enemies even the wakest ones are BS because that sword does the absolut minimum damge but ehh like all extraction games there all unfun boring and dumb not gonna be getting the full game this is atrocous for even a play test

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to try Reave and leave a review. We are not the development team behind Echo VR sorry. Reave is still in active development and this playtest is focused on gathering feedback about difficulty, balance, starting gear, and overall feel. Everyone starts weak and with nothing, it's about the journey to excellence overtime. Progression is key.

The starting equipment is intentionally light as you progress and level up by completing quests to get stronger and find better loot to do more damage and increase survivability.

If you are open to it, we would love to hear more specifics from you in our Discord so we can understand exactly where the difficulty spikes felt unfair. There are also tutorials and tips for getting started on the Discord too. Your feedback helps shape the direction of the game.

Thank you again for giving it a try

Fix your stupid game and remove stuff like the finger thing that is just there to take up room that could be used to improve the quality of the  game

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. Reave is currently in early Limited Access playtesting and is not a finished or final product. This is a free test phase where we are actively developing the game alongside the community, which means many systems are still incomplete or being iterated on.
Party members are intended to spawn together. If that did not happen, that is a bug and we would really appreciate it being reported so we can investigate and fix it. Please join our Discord at discord.gg/Reave and submit a bug report so the team can look into it.
Blocking does work, but if you attempt to block with no stamina remaining you will still take damage. There are short tutorials and guides on the Discord that explain stamina, blocking timing, and early combat mechanics in more detail.
Character customization is not in the game yet. It is planned for next year and is one of many features that are still in development. The current character is a placeholder, which is expected at this stage of development.
The extraction system, entry costs, enemies, visuals, interactions like doors, and hand and finger physics are all systems that are actively being tested and refined. Feedback on these mechanics is useful, but please keep in mind that they are not representative of the final experience.
Reave is designed to be learned with guidance and teamwork. Many players find the experience much smoother after watching the tutorials and teaming up with others in the community. Going in without that context can make the game feel far harsher than intended at this stage.
We appreciate feedback, but reviews written as if this were a finished, paid release do not reflect what this playtest is meant to be. If you are open to giving it another try, we strongly recommend joining the Discord, checking the tutorials, and reporting any bugs you encounter so we can improve the game together.
